Tarpon Springs isn’t just cute — it’s called Florida’s Little Greece for a reason. 🇬🇷🌴

In the early 1900s, Greek immigrants (mostly from the island of Kalymnos) settled here to work in the natural sponge-diving industry, and they turned this little Gulf Coast town into the sponge capital of the world. A lot of their families are still here today, which is why you’ll see Greek flags, hear Greek spoken on the docks, and eat food that tastes like it came straight from Athens.

The heart of it all is the Sponge Docks — where boats still bring in real sea sponges, shops sell them fresh off the boat, and you can watch sponge divers tell their stories. It’s one of the most unique waterfronts in Florida.

What to do while you’re here:
• Walk the Sponge Docks and check out the shops
• Take a sponge-diving boat tour
• Eat way too much at places like Hellas, Mykonos, or Mr. Souvlaki
• Grab Greek pastries and coffee
• Visit St. Nicholas Greek Orthodox Cathedral
• Walk by the water or hop on a sunset cruise
